Brown Rust.meaning: Brown Rust is a fungal disease that affects wheat plants, causing reddish-brown pustules on the leaves and stems.
Brown Rust.causes: Caused by the fungus Puccinia triticina under warm, humid conditions.
Brown Rust.symptoms: Small, reddish-brown pustules (uredinia) appear on the upper surface of leaves, leading to yellowing and drying of the affected areas.
Brown Rust.remedy: Can be minimized by planting resistant wheat varieties, using fungicides, and practicing crop rotation.

Healthy.meaning: Healthy wheat plant without any disease.
Healthy.symptoms: Normal plant appearance, with no unusual spots or wilting.

Yellow Rust.meaning: Yellow Rust is a fungal disease that affects wheat plants, causing yellow pustules on the leaves.
Yellow Rust.causes: Caused by the fungus Puccinia striiformis under cooler, moist conditions.
Yellow Rust.symptoms: Yellow-orange pustules appear in narrow stripes on the upper surface of leaves, leading to yellowing and reduced photosynthesis.
Yellow Rust.remedy: Can be controlled with fungicides, planting resistant varieties, and practicing crop rotation.
